text: ## Achievements & Awards James's achievements include the 2025 Physics Bowl National Silver Award, a national top 5% placement in China Thinks Big, publication in the Curieux Academic Journal, participation in the Lumiere Research Program, and participation in the 丘成桐中学科学奖 (Qiu Competition).

## Curieux Academic Journal publication Research paper on Large Language Models (LLMs) was published in the Curieux Academic Journal.
## Lumiere Research Program Participated in the Lumiere Research Program conducting independent research on LLM hallucinations.
## Physics Bowl National Silver Award Earned the National Silver Award in the 2025 Physics Bowl (during 10th grade). International physics competition.
## 丘成桐中学科学奖 (Qiu Competition) Participated in the 丘成桐中学科学奖 (Qiu Competition) with a paper on histology patch classification and neural network architectures.

text: James's highest rank in Apex Legends is Diamond 2, which he reached in Season 22.
## Age 17 years old

James introduces himself on his website: Hi! I'm a 17-year-old student living in Shanghai. I'm passionate about technology, and I enjoy spending my time exploring a mix of digital and real-world interests.

James introduces himself on his website: I love gaming, especially FPS titles like Apex Legends and Overwatch. Outside of gaming, I'm heavily involved in sports such as ice hockey, tennis, and floorball. You'll also probably find me enjoying ice cream or anything apple-flavored—juice, pie, you name it.
James Sui is a 17-year-old Student · Photographer · Athlete · Technologist living in Shanghai, China. Tagline: Tech, Motion, and Perspective..
## Full name James Sui
## Hometown Born and raised in Shanghai. Has lived there all his life (excluding vacations).
## Location Shanghai, China

## Personal challenge Describes being an IB student in China as the hardest thing in his life—endless assignments, tight deadlines, and being a non-domestic transfer student in a bilingual school. Reflects on massive improvement over time.

## Personal philosophy Wants to "democratize technology" through software design to minimize energy waste in low-income homes or through the creation of open-source hardware affordable to any family. Believes "the best role of an engineer is such that innovation can benefit the numerous and not the few."
## Role Student · Photographer · Athlete · Technologist
## Self-description James describes himself as "a 17-year-old student living in Shanghai" who is passionate about technology and enjoys exploring a mix of digital and real-world interests.
## Tagline "Tech, Motion, and Perspective."
## Views on talent vs hard work Believes hard work is more important than talent for long-term achievement, but that hard work must be accompanied by smart choices.

James wrote an essay titled 'Benchmarking Neural Classifiers for Medical Imaging'. This paper benchmarks multiple classifier architectures for medical image classification using histology embeddings. Comparing attention pooling, KNN, and MLP models, it evaluates accuracy, efficiency, and robustness. Results show attention pooling performs best overall, while simpler models remain competitive, informing practical deployment in healthcare settings.

metadata.title: Benchmarking Neural Classifiers for Medical Imaging
metadata.heading_path: Benchmarking Neural Classifiers for Medical Imaging

text: James wrote an essay titled 'Hallucinations in Large Language Models'. This study investigates hallucinations in large language models, examining why they occur and how they relate to model size. Using Gemma-3 models and the SimpleQA benchmark, it shows that scaling parameters alone is insufficient, highlighting the need for architectural improvements and stronger mitigation strategies.

text: ## On AI optimism "As we navigate the quirks and limitations of generative AI, we are not just users but pioneers in a new frontier of learning and productivity."

## Professional gamer as role model Would want to interview a professional gamer. Admires how their spirit and discipline can inspire his own fields.

## Sees failure as data "Failure is nothing more than a piece of data that will result in a more appropriate design."

## Values democratizing technology "My aim is to democratize technology, through the software design to minimize waste of energy in low-income homes or through the creation of open-source hardware affordable to any families."

## Values friendship above material things "Time spent with worthwhile friends are always the most enjoyable." Would use unlimited money to buy a gaming computer for a friend who was refused one by his parents.

## Views engineering as duality "The best part about engineering is that there is duality in it; it is filled with creative design but yet also requires strong logic and is extremely rigorous."

## 3D modeling journey Started 3D modeling in 6th grade. Self-taught SolidWorks. Describes the process as challenging at times, especially when communicating design intent through AI tools. Building 3D models can be frustrating but rewarding.

## AI visual recognition project (summer) Described as his most important project: AI models designed for visual recognition. Worked on this during summer, combining computer vision with deep learning.

## 智愈APP (Zhiyu App) — Flutter medical platform Cross-platform tumor patient recovery management platform. Features: patient portal (medication management, symptom tracking, medical records), doctor portal (patient management, prescriptions, appointments, chat), AI assistant (smart Q&A, report interpretation). Full-stack: Flutter/Dart, Node.js/Express/MongoDB, JWT auth, OpenAI API, Firebase push notifications.

text: ## Blockchain / Solidity Uses Foundry (Ethereum development toolkit) for Uniswap V3 Extended Essay experiment. Working with Solidity smart contracts.
## Econ Grapher Python package for drawing economic graphs using the turtle library. Includes elements for Axes, Lines, Labels, and Tags.
## Engineering tools SolidWorks, AutoCAD, Ls-Dyna Dynamic 3D Finite Element Computational Analysis.
## Flappy Bird game Simple Python flappy bird game built with Pygame (collaborated with a classmate).

## Foundation Model Histology Benchmark Benchmarks classifier architectures (attention pooling, KNN, MLP) on histology patch embeddings from eight foundation models (Hibou-B, Hibou-L, Phikon, Phikonv2, UNI, UNI v2, Virchow, Virchow2) using NCTCRCHE100K dataset.
## FTC Robotics Member of a newly created FTC (FIRST Tech Challenge) robotics team. Designed, built, and tested competition robots.
## GitHub activity GitHub username: Cheezecats (https://github.com/Cheezecats). 55 contributions in the last year (as of July 2026), with activity in 2024, 2025, and 2026.
## Hallucination Evaluation Personal project detecting LLM hallucinations using the SimpleQA benchmark (by Jason Wei and OpenAI, Nov 2024). Tests on Gemma models (1B, 4B, 12B parameters).
## ML / AI research Conducted independent research on neural network architectures and LLM hallucinations. Uses Python with PyTorch for ML work.
## PC build project Built a PC from scratch using purchased hardware. Motivated by wanting to watch hardware engineering videos and play Fortnite. Sparked by interest in Nvidia and graphics cards.

## Personal website (cheezecats.github.io) Built from scratch with Vite + React 19 + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS v4 + Framer Motion. Features: cinematic photographic hero, frosted-glass nav, bento showcase grid, multi-page routes (photography gallery, videos, hobbies timeline, essays), light-first theme with dark toggle, and p5.js generative flow-field accent. Revamped from static HTML to this stack in June 2026.

## Projects & Skills James's projects include a React/TypeScript personal website, an FFT guitar tuner, a Flutter medical recovery platform, an economics graphing tool, a SAT vocabulary app, an LLM hallucination evaluator, a histology classifier benchmark, a Uniswap V3 research experiment, an FTC robotics project, and a Flappy Bird game.
## Programming languages Python, C, C++, TypeScript, Dart (Flutter), Solidity. Self-taught Python during middle school.

## SAT Vocabulary Review App Quizlet-style SAT vocabulary review app with 1000 words, built with React 18 + TypeScript + Vite + Tailwind CSS. Features flashcards, bookmarks, spaced repetition system (New → Learning → Reviewing → Mastered stages).

## Sensor project Intercoordinating sensor project to monitor environment for harmful gases. Interest in working with hardware. Described as the most valuable thing done over summer — combining hardware and software skills.

## Tongji University research project (summer 2024) Research project at Tongji University in Shanghai. Topic: anti-collision effectiveness of cellular-structured materials / how graded materials absorb energy. Tools: SolidWorks, AutoCAD, Ls-Dyna. Outcome: national top 5% in CTB competition.

## Tune-app (FFT Guitar Tuner) Browser-based FFT Frequency Analyzer built for IB HL Physics Internal Assessment. Investigates relationship between string gauge, tension, and fundamental frequency in guitar strings. Uses Web Audio API, real-time FFT analysis, autocorrelation, and Harmonic Product Spectrum (HPS) for pitch detection.

## Uniswap V3 EE experiment Foundry-based simulation experiment for Uniswap V3 Extended Essay. Smart contracts: MockPool.sol, MathHelpers.sol, ResearchExperiment.t.sol. Tests pool behavior including swaps, mints, and burns with varying concentration ranges.

## Web development Built personal website (cheezecats.github.io) from scratch using HTML/CSS, TypeScript, Vite, React, Tailwind CSS, Framer Motion. Hosted on GitHub Pages with CI/CD via GitHub Actions.

James plays Floorball, which he started in 2023. Floorball brought a fresh perspective to my sporting life when I joined my school's varsity team. I originally saw it as a way to keep my hockey skills sharp during boarding, but I soon found myself captivated by the game's distinct flow.

I originally saw it as a way to keep my hockey skills sharp during boarding, but I soon found myself captivated by the game's distinct flow. Transitioning from the ice to the court required a quick study of new mechanics, and I've loved every bit of the learning curve.

James plays Ice Hockey, which he started in 2015. My journey with ice hockey began at a young age. The speed, strategy, and teamwork required in this sport excites me every time I play. I have been actively involved in local leagues and have participated in numerous training sessions both abroad (America, Japan, Russia) and domestically.

James plays Skiing, which he started in 2013. Skiing and snowboarding is a winter passion that I look forward to every year and also an important family tradition. The thrill of gliding down snowy slopes and the serene beauty of winter landscapes never fail to captivate me.

The thrill of gliding down snowy slopes and the serene beauty of winter landscapes never fail to captivate me. From carving turns on groomed trails to exploring powder-filled backcountry, and park riding, skiing always keeps me excited and challenged.

James plays Tennis, which he started in 2018. Tennis started as a sport I can play in my community with my friends and family. Moving forwards, other than practising every week, I started to attend local tournaments to improve my skill. The individual nature of tennis is both challenging and rewarding — a completely different experience from team sports.

## Writing & Essays James has written essays and research papers about LLM hallucinations, medical imaging and histology classifiers, Uniswap V3, and an IB Physics guitar tuner project.

## Combined research paper (丘竞赛) "How Changing Neural Network Architectures Improves Performance: Insights from LLM Hallucination, Simple Classification, and Vision Tasks" - a combined paper addressing three research questions: (1) How architecture changes affect LLM hallucination vs parameter scaling, (2) Performance gains in classification tasks, (3) CNNs vs ViTs in visual inspection.

## Extended Essay (EE) Interdisciplinary CS + Economics essay on Uniswap V3, using Foundry simulation to study capital efficiency, slippage, and impermanent loss with varying concentration ranges and trade sizes.

## Histology classification research paper "Building the Best Pipeline for Histology Patch Classification" - benchmarks multiple classifier architectures (attention pooling, KNN, MLP) on histology patch embeddings from eight foundation models (Virchow, Virchow2, UNI, UNIv2, Hibou-B, Hibou-L, Phikon, Phikonv2) using the NCTCRCHE100K dataset. Attention pooling on patch tokens consistently achieves best F1 scores.
## IB Physics IA FFT guitar tuner (tune-app) investigating the relationship between string gauge, tension, and fundamental frequency in guitar strings.

## LLM Hallucination research paper "The Hallucination Problem in LLMs: Why Scaling Parameters Isn't Enough" - investigates hallucinations in large language models, examining why they occur and how they relate to model size. Uses Gemma-3 models (1B-12B parameters) on the SimpleQA benchmark (4,326 QA pairs). Results: Gemma-1B hallucination rate 97.11%, Gemma-4B 95.19%, Gemma-12B 93.85%. Published in the Curieux Academic Journal.
## Lumiere Research Program Participated in the Lumiere Research Program, conducting independent research on LLM hallucinations. Submitted milestone documents and a final research paper.
## Math IA Markov chain packet loss model applied to Apex Legends (20Hz tick rate), with MLE matrices and cross-game comparison to League of Legends and Valorant.

## NYT "Growing up with AI" submission Submitted a piece to the New York Times "Growing up with AI" contest about the frustration and hope of switching between different AI models. Describes chaotic search histories, memory retention issues between models, and AI as "a necessary step in building the muscle memory for the complex, multi-tool future of work and learning."

## TOK Exhibition IA Prompt #14: "Does some knowledge belong only to particular communities of knowers?" Using three objects: Charge Rifle (Apex Legends), MyGO CD (春日影), and Raspberry Pi. Explores tacit knowledge, linguistic barriers, and community-constructed knowledge.
